title: Evidence-graded Python symbol call graph v1
goal: Produce a deterministic Python call-site artifact with explicit S0/S1 evidence, strict provenance, conservative lexical resolution, bounded caller/callee/reference consumers and read-only MCP wiring.

acceptance.assertion: Every Python call site is recorded deterministically; only unambiguous statically bound targets become S1 resolved relations, while shadowed, dynamic, foreign and ambiguous calls remain explicit S0 evidence.
Parameters, local assignments, nested definitions, decorators, default arguments and class-definition expressions cannot be misattributed to a module function or the function being defined.
The call graph has a Draft-07 schema, coherent bundle role metadata, run and canonical dump binding, integrity validation, public-share exclusion and machine-readable negative semantics.
find_references, get_callers and get_callees return bounded path, range, caller and target evidence, reject ambiguous symbol selection and attach live freshness without refreshing a snapshot.
Focused and full Lenskit tests, Ruff, repository ratchets and exact-head GitHub CI pass; a diff-bound self-review records correctness, regression, security and integration findings.

metadata.source_request: User requested all Symbol Call Graph work to be registered and implemented.
metadata.outcome: Lenskit now emits a deterministic evidence-graded Python Call Graph v1, binds callers to exact symbol definition ranges, exposes bounded read-only find_references/get_callers/get_callees MCP tools, and keeps ambiguous or dynamic relations as explicit S0 evidence.
validation: Exact-head maintainability ratchet passed with no findings.
Exact-head pytest excluding browser/live-doc tests passed: 4102 passed, 1 skipped, 13 deselected.
Exact-head release contract passed with no findings.
Focused MCP, bundle and navigation tests passed: 99 passed.
Recursion and caller-identity negative tests passed: 31 passed.
Two independent release-candidate builds were byte-identical and verified.
All 15 GitHub push workflows on Lenskit main merge commit 48d1797553ddb233a9b64b22e50436ca5c6ac291 completed successfully.
Bureau Doctor has no finding for RPU-V1-T023; its global unhealthy state is caused by pre-existing unrelated runtime/state-root findings and broad-lease warnings.

verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t023-consumers: find_references, get_callers and get_callees are bounded read-only consumers with path/range identity, exact symbol selection and live freshness reporting.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t023-contract: Draft-07 contract, bundle role, canonical dump/run binding, integrity checks, public-share exclusion and explicit non-claims are implemented.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t023-producer: Every parsed Python call site is deterministic; unique safe local bindings become S1, while shadowed, dynamic, foreign, redefined and ambiguous calls remain S0.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t023-scopes: Regression tests cover parameters, assignments, nested definitions, decorators, defaults, class expressions, methods, recursion and duplicate definition ranges.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t023-validation: Local full gates, diff-bound self-review, all PR checks and all 15 post-merge main workflows passed on the exact implementation head and merge commit.

non_claims: merged PR evidence does not prove deployment
merged PR evidence does not prove dependent tasks are complete
merged PR evidence does not prove runtime correctness
